fresh_facts.claim: Two US carrier strike groups (USS Gerald R. Ford and USS Abraham Lincoln) are operating in the Middle East region as of March 7, 2026, with the Ford repositioned rapidly amid escalating tensions.
USS George H.W. Bush completed preparations for Eastern Mediterranean deployment as of March 7, 2026.
Vermont Air National Guard's 158th Fighter Wing was deployed to the Middle East on March 3, 2026.
Operation Epic Fury involved US strikes on over 1,000 Iranian targets in the first 24 hours starting around March 1, 2026, using air, naval, and cyber operations.
Iran launched ballistic missiles and drones that penetrated US and partner defenses, hitting a US tactical operations center and killing 4 Americans.
US and partner air defenses including Patriot and THAAD systems intercepted hundreds of Iranian missiles and drones.
Pentagon briefing on March 2, 2026 confirmed no US boots on the ground in Iran at that time.
President Trump has not ruled out ground operations in Iran, potentially including National Guard deployment, as of March 2, 2026.
President Masoud Pezeshkian's statements regarding Iranian attacks on March 7, 2026 attracted criticism from hardline officials, revealing significant divisions within Iran's power structure.
The Assembly of Experts is expected to convene to select a successor to the Supreme Leader, with potential candidates including Khamenei's son.
The IRGC is advocating for Khamenei's son's appointment as Supreme Leader successor, though resistance remains from other senior officials.
CENTCOM reported over 3,000 strikes in Iran since February 28, 2026.
The IDF reported hitting over 300 targets in Iran in recent days as of March 7, 2026.
Iranian drone attacks caused damage to Saudi Arabian civilian infrastructure, including closure of the kingdom's largest domestic oil refinery, as of March 7, 2026.
Ukraine's President Zelensky offered assistance to Saudi Arabia in countering the Iranian drone threat on March 7, 2026.
The third round of indirect talks between the US and Iran took place in Geneva on February 26, 2026, mediated by Oman's Foreign Minister Badr Al Busaidi.
American negotiators left Geneva disappointed after indirect talks on February 26, 2026, while Iranian officials and Omani mediators expressed more optimism about significant progress.
The US State Department declared Iran a state sponsor of wrongful detention on February 27, 2026 and evacuated embassies from Iran.
The US coordinated military operations with regional allies including Qatar, UAE, Kuwait, Jordan, and Saudi Arabia as of March 2, 2026.

regime_fall.current_status: Operation Epic Fury has delivered over 3,000 cumulative strikes across Iranian military infrastructure since February 28, with three carrier strike groups (Ford, Lincoln, and Bush) now operationally positioned, while the regime faces critical internal divisions following Supreme Leader Khamenei's death on March 5, contested succession of his son Mojtaba, deepening economic collapse, and persistent internet blackout now in its 10th day.
regime_fall.last_major_event: Assembly of Experts named Mojtaba Khamenei as new Supreme Leader on March 9, 2026, following Ali Khamenei's death on March 5, while sustained multi-front military pressure continues with over 3,000 strikes reported by CENTCOM and 300+ by IDF since February 28.
